notes: BG 2.25 closes the imperishable block with three properties (avyakta, acintya, avikārya) and the therefore-clause (tasmād evaṁ viditvā nānuśocitum arhasi). The engineering layer preserves all three properties as distinct: unmanifest (cannot be indicated like an implementation), unthinkable in implementation terms (cannot be apprehended by tracing through any single service), unchangeable (modification of implementation does not modify contract). Shankara's deductive-chain reading (beyond senses → beyond thought → beyond change) is honoured by the cumulative ordering. Ramanuja's kind-distinction reading (the Self is different in kind from objects pramāṇas manifest) is honoured by the framing 'specification consists of what persists across implementations, not what any single implementation embodies.' The therefore-clause is preserved precisely: knowing the contract layer, perform no mourning ritual. The closing instruction is correctly understood as the close of the entire imperishable block (2.20-2.25). PASS.
